title: Two-step extraction-then-analysis separation
description: Extraction (expensive, done once) produces service-agnostic JSON. Analysis (cheap, done many times) runs arbitrary LLM prompts against extracted metadata. Key cost optimization pattern.

title: Multi-granularity output hierarchy: frames → shots → scenes
description: Three levels of temporal granularity. Frames (individual images with ML features). Shots (continuous camera sequences detected via similarity threshold). Scenes (narrative segments from LLM grouping of shots).

title: Smart sampling via multimodal embedding dedup
description: Two implementations: OpenSearch-backed (cloud) and FAISS-based (local). Titan Multimodal Embedding generates frame vectors, cosine similarity identifies duplicates. Claims 50% frame reduction.

title: Independently toggleable per-frame ML features
description: Each extraction feature (labels, text, celebrity, moderation, caption) independently enabled/disabled via config. Adding new features = adding new Lambda to Step Functions iteration.

title: Cognito auth + API Gateway with presigned URLs
description: Standard AWS auth pattern. Presigned URLs for S3 video upload/download. No custom auth logic.

title: No error recovery or retry in extraction pipeline
description: Lambda functions have basic try/catch but no DLQ, retry logic, or partial extraction recovery. Failed frame extraction = lost data for that frame.

title: Single test file in tests/ directory
description: 163 files, 52 Python files, 1 test file. No CI pipeline visible. No unit tests for extraction logic.

title: 30 commits, 4 contributors, last updated Feb 2025
description: Low development velocity. 5 months since last commit. Typical of AWS reference architecture — built, published, maintenance-mode.

title: Comprehensive README with architecture diagrams and cost estimates
description: README includes architecture overview, deployment steps, cost breakdowns ($0.16-0.35/min), feature configuration screenshots. Good reference documentation.
